name: Ann Roe
subject: Don't let Trump and his allies rewrite history
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ANN ROE FOR CONGRESS Ann Roe for Congress
body:
|
---
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40418171/324255277/-766995749?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTY2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzMjZiYmZiOS0wOTZmLWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDkyM2MiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=rygmdjC2CLF2ONIYisZjpqKW6gME0WFSsW-kgGp859U=&emci=86d9d6c1-a16d-ec11-94f6-c896650d923c&emdi=326bbfb9-096f-ec11-94f6-c896650d923c&ceid=15639879)

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40418173/324255279/-1425244157?refcode=em22_220106_jan6&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTY2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzMjZiYmZiOS0wOTZmLWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDkyM2MiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=rygmdjC2CLF2ONIYisZjpqKW6gME0WFSsW-kgGp859U=)
---
Friends,

One year ago today, I had cleared my calendar to watch Congress certify the results of the 2020 election. I don't need to tell you that what I saw -- what we _all_ saw -- was not the simple formality we've witnessed at the end of every election cycle.

What we actually saw was chaos and destruction from far-right extremists draped in American flags claiming to be "patriots", committing nothing short of treason. They stormed past Capitol Police and infiltrated our Capitol carrying nooses and looting whatever they could find.

As the events unfolded, I switched among news channels, and newspaper live updates and I was sick. Tears came to my eyes as I watched the smoke, the stampede, the lawmakers hiding in their offices or huddling together in the corner of the room. **What we watched was an attack not on a building or an election, but on democracy itself.**

Friends, democracy is not a guarantee. It is a living, breathing concept represented in tangible form by elected representatives, each and every voter, as well as the Capitol that itself stands as a recognized symbol. **That day, We the People were attacked.**

And what's come of it? Make no mistake -- this attack wasn't unsuccessful, it's ongoing. Trump and other bad-faith actors have been spinning this story since day one, painting those involved not as rioters, but as heroes. Nearly every Republican in Congress voted against launching the Committee to Investigate the January 6th Attack, including our very own Bryan Steil.

**_Bryan, why don't you want us to investigate?_**

Those responsible MUST be held accountable and brought to justice. If we don't act, the next attack won't dissipate in a matter of hours -- it'll stay for generations.

**Doing nothing is being complicit.**

\-- Ann Roe

name: Eric M. Lynn
subject: A dark anniversary - a year later
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Lynn for Congress
body:
Let’s stand together and recognize just how fragile our democracy is https://secure.actblue.com/donate/el-jan6 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/el-jan6]Dear friends,
Today is the one year anniversary of the January 6th Insurrection at the United States Capitol.
Almost 20 years ago, I was a staffer in Congress and evacuated from the Capitol on 9/11 as our nation was under attack. Watching the halls of Congress being invaded by people intending to do harm to our Representatives of both parties, and to deface the institution of our government is what motivated me to run for Congress in 2022. My hope is that such an event never happens again.
Right-wing extremists are the greatest threat to our future, and unfortunately, the mainstream right wing media apparatus has enabled and empowered them for far too long. Threats of violence, claims of stealing elections, and bigotry have no place in the United States.
We must work constantly to root out the causes of these dangerous ideologies.
The Congressional Investigation of January 6th is ongoing. I’m shocked that elected Members of Congress are suspected of aiding the attackers, just as I will forever be ashamed and angered that a President of the United States encouraged such actions.
We cannot become numb to the harsh realities that were highlighted by January 6, and we need to take these threats seriously.
Like other pivotal points in our history, I believe that all Americans will remember where they were when they heard the disturbing news. We must use these memories to focus on the need to protect our democratic values and ensure that our institutions are defended from extremists.
I’ll always stand on the side of our democracy and democratic institutions, and I hope to see progress to re-enforce our values and bring the January 6th attackers to justice.
In solidarity,
Eric Lynn

name: Laura Hernandez Holmes
subject: NEW: now endorsed by the Progressives in Congress!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Greg Casar for Congress
body:
We’ve got some huge news we wanted to share with you first, Peter!
Today the Congressional Progressive Caucus PAC endorsed our campaign for TX-35, the first endorsement of a new candidate in the 2022 cycle!
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/greg-casar-for-congress?refcode=01.06-email&amount=35 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/greg-casar-for-congress?refcode=01.06-email&amount=35 ]This is a big deal, Peter. The progressive caucus is a group of nearly 100 sitting members of Congress and is led by fantastic officials like Representatives Pramila Jayapal, Jamie Raskin, and Mark Pocan. They’ve helped elect so many progressive leaders—and now they’re supporting our campaign.
Can you join progressives in Congress who are supporting our campaign? A $20 or $25 contribution will help us reach voters across the 35th district. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/greg-casar-for-congress?refcode=01.06-email&amount=35 ]
Donate! [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/greg-casar-for-congress?refcode=01.06-email&amount=35 ]Progressives are leading the charge to pass the Build Back Better Act that would invest in working families. And they’re fighting back against the right wing assault on voting rights and reproductive rights.
We’re building a movement of folks from San Antonio to Austin who are ready to fight for Medicare for All, good jobs, reproductive rights, and a functional electric grid. We’re proud to have earned the endorsement of a caucus led by some of our greatest progressive champions, and we’re ready to get to work alongside them to fight and deliver for Texas working families.
But to get there, we need your help. With less than six weeks until early voting starts, we need to reach as many voters as possible—and your support will help us do that. Pitch in $20 or $25 today to help us get out the vote. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/greg-casar-for-congress?refcode=01.06-email&amount=35 ]
With the support of progressives in Congress and people like you, we can win this race and put working families first.
Thank you,
-Laura

name: Adam Schiff
subject: some thoughts
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by Schiff for Congress
body:
I’m in the Capitol today for events to commemorate the violent insurrection of one year ago. And I didn’t want to let the day get away from me without sending you some thoughts.

I remember the January 6th attack on the Capitol like it was yesterday.

“Please grab a mask!” a Capitol Police officer shouted, referring to the gas masks under each chair in the House chamber. “Be prepared to don your mask in the event the room is breached... Just be prepared. Stay calm.”

There is nothing like being told to stay calm to get one’s heart beating faster. Verbal shouting matches broke out on the House Floor and Gallery. One of my Democratic colleagues yelled at Republicans, “This is because of you!” The reply from a Republican was a defensive and succinct, “shut up!” Recriminations spread as we waited for police to secure an escape route.

The din of the insurrectionists grew louder as the minutes dragged on. That’s when I heard it. A tremendous thud — something had been thrust against the doors not twenty yards away from me, battering them. Thud . A moment later, again: thud .

“You need to get out!” a police officer shouted, and directed us to a set of open doors. “Move!”

“You can’t let them see you,” one of the Republican members told me. He said that he knew these people, that he could talk to them, but that I was in a whole different category. He was right. If the insurrectionists broke through the doors, I would likely be one of the first they would come after.

I was touched by the evident concern of these GOP members. But that emotion rapidly gave way to another. If they hadn’t been pushing the Big Lie about our elections, I wouldn’t need to worry over my safety. None of us would.

They were insurrectionists, too, these members in suits and ties, sworn to uphold the constitution but unwilling to honor their oaths. After brave police officers finally cleared the building and it was safe for us to return to complete the joint session, a majority of Republican members STILL voted to overturn the election.

In the wake of that awful day, I hoped that we might turn a corner, that Republicans would come to repudiate Trump and Trumpism, after seeing the terrible devastation it brought to our country and democracy. Instead, in the days, weeks, months, and year since, they have doubled down on the Big Lie and used it to attack the work of nonpartisan elections officials around the country and the right to vote.

It is all too tragically the case, that our democracy is at greater risk today, than it was one year ago. Our Capitol building may be better protected, but the democracy it stands for is not. We must awaken to the threat.

I hope the anniversary of January 6th will inspire a renewed commitment to the cherished ideals of our founders, that we will rededicate ourselves to the great experiment in self-governance that they conceived, and strengthen its protections.

We will get through this difficult time in our history. America is a deeply resilient country filled with patriotic citizens who love our democracy, and those who do, vastly outnumber those trying to tear it down.

I put my faith in the optimism of our founders. I hope you will too.

Please take care of yourselves, and thank you for all your support during these trying times.

— Adam

---

Paid for and authorized by Schiff for Congress
Schiff for Congress
150 E. Olive Ave.
Suite 208
Burbank, CA 91502
United States

name: Michelle Siri
subject: I am incredibly honored
disclaimer_text: Paid for by John King for Governor, Eliza Leighton, Treasurer
body:
We have the chance to build a Maryland that works for everyone, and it’s going to take all of us to make it happen.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40419575/324261768/-1613255896?refcode=BLU-KING-20220106-1a-EM&&&&amount=25&refcode2=21a7e077-0c6f-ec11-94f6-c896650d923c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDE0NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMWE3ZTA3Ny0wYzZmLWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDkyM2MiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KndbbGdu-uLsFu-kkmy9Fv3LX7n3inDDp9TYQsDs2dY=)
---
| Peter,

**My name is Michelle Siri, and I am so honored to be joining this ticket with Secretary John King's campaign for governor of Maryland.**

As a first-generation Iranian-American, my mom always instilled in me the importance of never taking our rights for granted and always advocating for those around me, and it’s because of her and my own life experience that I’ve dedicated my life working towards achieving equal rights for women here in Maryland and across the country — through my law career, at Planned Parenthood, or at the Women’s Law Center. **Now, I have the chance to do it on an even bigger scale as a part of this campaign with John.**

From the moment I met John, it was clear that his authenticity, honesty, and commitment to serving others are unlike anyone I had met before. As Secretary of Education under President Obama, the President of the Education Trust, and the founder of Strong Future Maryland, he worked tirelessly to advance bold legislation that invests in our public schools, protects workers, and safeguards our environment. **Across Maryland and the country, John has created opportunities and changed lives at every level. Now, he’s ready to lead our state — and I’m humbled by the opportunity to get to work building a more equitable Maryland alongside him.**
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40419576/324261769/-1613255896?refcode=BLU-KING-20220106-1a-EM&&&&amount=25&refcode2=21a7e077-0c6f-ec11-94f6-c896650d923c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDE0NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMWE3ZTA3Ny0wYzZmLWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDkyM2MiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KndbbGdu-uLsFu-kkmy9Fv3LX7n3inDDp9TYQsDs2dY=)

I’m not a politician, Peter. I’m just a proud Marylander with 20 years of experience advocating for legislation and pushing for women’s rights on the state and national levels.

I’m grateful for the chance to introduce myself, and I look forward to sharing more of my story and vision for Maryland with you over the next few months. **We have the chance to build a Maryland that works for everyone, and it’s going to take all of us to make it happen.** [_**So can John and I count on you to show your support for our ticket and help us start the new year off strong by making a donation today?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40419578/324261771/-1613255896?refcode=BLU-KING-20220106-1a-EM&&&&amount=25&refcode2=21a7e077-0c6f-ec11-94f6-c896650d923c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDE0NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMWE3ZTA3Ny0wYzZmLWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDkyM2MiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KndbbGdu-uLsFu-kkmy9Fv3LX7n3inDDp9TYQsDs2dY=)
| [DONATE ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40419579/324261772/-1613255896?refcode=BLU-KING-20220106-1a-EM&&&&amount=25&refcode2=21a7e077-0c6f-ec11-94f6-c896650d923c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDE0NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMWE3ZTA3Ny0wYzZmLWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDkyM2MiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KndbbGdu-uLsFu-kkmy9Fv3LX7n3inDDp9TYQsDs2dY=)
---
I am grateful for any support you can give, Peter. I am honored that John asked me to join this strong, progressive team and I know together we have the incredible opportunity to build a more equitable Maryland and a more just future for all. **Let’s get to work.**

Grateful to be a part of this movement,
Michelle Siri
Democrat for Lieutenant Governor of Maryland

subject: We have to fight for our democracy

body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40419837/324263276/69228752?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZmNiNDFlOS0wZDZmLWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDkyM2MiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Y-fsA8G8WJpITQfqVY9JEAwXHGNs1HNUO03Ws94y6fI=&emci=e00329dc-576e-ec11-94f6-c896650d923c&emdi=afcb41e9-0d6f-ec11-94f6-c896650d923c&ceid=15353729)

Peter,

One year ago today, we watched as the nation's Capitol was attacked by people trying to destroy our democracy. But events like this weren't just happening in Washington, D.C. -- here in Oregon, we were still dealing with the aftermath of a similar attack on our own Capitol just weeks before.

**Here's the thing we want to remember: We may want to move forward, but we should also reflect and honor what was lost and what remains.**

When our democracy is under attack, we have to fight for it.

Today is a day to remember those who lost their lives defending our democracy last year. Across the country, we were faced with a kind of violence that no one could have expected. But, together, we prevailed.

And Tina prevailed too. Mere weeks after the attack in Salem, Tina held her House colleague who let rioters into the Oregon Capitol building responsible – and he was later unanimously expelled from the House. She stood up for what was right.

That's why Tina is running.

She is a leader for democracy in and outside of Oregon -- because our democracy is worth defending.

body:

# [Arizona Crossing Sees 2,404.9% Hike in Illegal Immigrants Over Last Year](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57570&L=26554&F=H&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvYXJpem9uYS1jcm9zc2luZy1zZWVzLTI0MDQtOS1oaWtlLWluLWlsbGVnYWwtaW1taWdyYW50cy1vdmVyLWxhc3QteWVhci8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj1jb3JydXB0aW9uK2Nocm9uaWNsZXMmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==)
As illegal immigration continues to slam American cities near the Mexican border, one Arizona town in particular is feeling the heat with a ghastly 2,404.9% increase in migrants during the first two months of fiscal year 2022—which started in October—compared to the same period last year. Situated around 10 miles from Mexico on the banks of the Colorado River, Yuma, in southwestern Arizona, began to see an unprecedented influx of illegal aliens in fiscal year 2021 along with the nation’s other Border Patrol sectors. In 2021 eight of the nine crossing stations along the southern border saw triple-digit percentage increases in illegal immigrants over the previous year, according to government figures. Yuma took the prize with an unbelievable 1,200.4% hike in apprehensions at the end of the fiscal year in September.
[Read More](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57570&L=26554&F=H&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvYXJpem9uYS1jcm9zc2luZy1zZWVzLTI0MDQtOS1oaWtlLWluLWlsbGVnYWwtaW1taWdyYW50cy1vdmVyLWxhc3QteWVhci8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj1jb3JydXB0aW9uK2Nocm9uaWNsZXMmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==)
|

| |

### [New York’s racialized coronavirus priorities are illegal and warrant DOJ investigation, critics say ](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=57570&L=26555&F=H&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvbnktcmFjaWFsaXplZC1jb3ZpZC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj1jb3JydXB0aW9uK2Nocm9uaWNsZXMmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==) Fox News New York is blatantly violating the Constitution and should be investigated by the Department of Justice (DOJ) for prioritizing non-White individuals for certain coronavirus treatments, critics told Fox News.

Tom Fitton, who leads the conservative group Judicial Watch, similarly told Fox News that the Justice Department should investigate the issue as it constitutes a form of illegal discrimination. clean_body: As illegal immigration continues to slam American cities near the Mexican border, one Arizona town in particular is feeling the heat with a ghastly 2,404.9% increase in migrants during the first two months of fiscal year 2022—which started in October—compared to the same period last year. | | | Because no one is above the law! | January 05, 2022 ---|---|--- | | == == As illegal immigration continues to slam American cities near the Mexican border, one Arizona town in particular is feeling the heat with a ghastly 2,404.9% increase in migrants during the first two months of fiscal year 2022—which started in October—compared to the same period last year. Situated around 10 miles from Mexico on the banks of the Colorado River, Yuma, in southwestern Arizona, began to see an unprecedented influx of illegal aliens in fiscal year 2021 along with the nation’s other Border Patrol sectors. In 2021 eight of the nine crossing stations along the southern border saw triple-digit percentage increases in illegal immigrants over the previous year, according to government figures. Yuma took the prize with an unbelievable 1,200.4% hike in apprehensions at the end of the fiscal year in September. == | | | == Fox News New York is blatantly violating the Constitution and should be investigated by the Department of Justice (DOJ) for prioritizing non-White individuals for certain coronavirus treatments, critics told Fox News. Tom Fitton, who leads the conservative group Judicial Watch, similarly told Fox News that the Justice Department should investigate the issue as it constitutes a form of illegal discrimination. == == Judicial Watch AG Garland testified that he based his memorandum off of a letter from the National School Board Association (NSBA) that labeled parents as “domestic terrorists.” The National School Boards Association later issued an apology for the letter. In November, Republicans on the House Judiciary Committee questioned the completeness of Garland’s testimony. == The Washington Times Judicial Watch President Tom Fitton said he was permanently banned from TikTok, the popular social media video platform with China-based ownership. The head of the conservative activist group said he was removed from TikTok without an explanation. = = = = == == | CLIPS | | | | | | Keep our investigators on the job uncovering the truth: | = | | | | ---|---|--- = = = Judicial Watch, Inc. 425 3rd St Sw Ste 800, Washington, DC 20024 202.646.5172 (C)2017-2022, All Rights Reserved = |

Friend, Today is a somber day as we remember what took place just one year ago: a violent insurrection against the U.S. Capitol at the hands of Donald Trump’s supporters. I know I’ll never forget watching the footage of thousands of angry people forming a mob with a premeditated plan to storm the Capitol, attacking police officers and threatening to harm members of Congress along the way, all in a desperate attempt to keep Trump in power. **In order to prevent this from ever happening again, we have to acknowledge and hold accountable those who incited and supported this attack: Trump and his loyal followers like NM-02’s Rep. Yvette Herrell, who voted against certifying the 2020 presidential election results and pushed dangerous conspiracy theories about election fraud.** Following this disturbing day, Herrell voted against impeaching Trump, and has continued to push extremist lies about the 2020 election along with her friends like Marjorie Taylor Greene, who still lie to their supporters that the election was stolen from them. **One year later, it’s clear that the future of our democracy depends on this November’s election.** If the GOP takes back the House or Senate and sends more extreme, QAnon-believers to Congress, the fate of our free and fair elections will be in grave jeopardy. name: Sara Jacobs
subject: what I saw on January 6, 2021
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sara Jacobs for Congress
body:
I will never shake the memory of being with my new colleagues in the House Chamber with no idea of what was going to happen next.

| Friend,

One year ago today, on my fourth day in Congress, our nation’s Capitol Building was attacked by violent insurrectionists who refused to accept the results of the 2020 presidential election.

On that day, I spent hours sheltering in an undisclosed location, having been told to remove any identifying items that would mark me as a member of Congress. I will never shake the memory of being with my new colleagues in the House Chamber with no idea of what was going to happen next. As I hid, scared for my life under the seats in the House Gallery, and climbing over handrails and seats with a gas mask on to escape the mob, I knew that the most important thing I could do was share the truth of what happened and hold anyone accountable who committed, incited, or encouraged those acts of violence.

I’ve spent my career working on conflict and political violence at the United Nations and the State Department. I’ve worked on post-coup transitions and responses to electoral violence and violent extremism in places like Fiji, Mali, Nigeria, Kenya, and the Democratic Republic of the Congo. The lessons from around the world are clear: The response to political violence must always be accountability. That is why I am still committed to holding accountable the people responsible for this attack.

In the year since the January 6th insurrection, many have crafted a narrative that this violence was the end of a tumultuous time in our nation. I believe that it was the beginning — **and no one can scare me enough to keep me from coming back to do the work to preserve and defend our democracy.**

I am endlessly grateful to the law enforcement officers who kept my colleagues and me safe that day, and for the solidarity amongst my colleagues that emerged from the attacks. Our “Gallery Group” shows the best of what Congress can be and helped me process the trauma we went through. Whether you experienced the violence firsthand or watched it on TV, we all experienced trauma that day, and I hope you are seeking out the help and care that you need to process that.

I am proud to work every day to restore trust in our democracy and prove that it can deliver on the biggest challenges facing us, and I am so grateful for your support.

Thank you,
Sara
---

body:
This is disqualifying

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40420687/324268762/-1987745334?refcode=jan6tests&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82OTQxOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3OTlhZTgxZC0xMjZmLWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDkyM2MiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=x0V51aF6hZKToIIlqZNidgUlgqe9lvWKBjsgpBy0a84=) Peter, For the past few weeks, people across Florida have waited in literally hours of long lines for COVID-19 testing. **Today, we learned that Ron DeSantis had been sitting on a stockpile of 1 million tests that have now expired. That’s 1 million COVID tests that could have been used to prevent the spread of the virus, and prevent the loss of life. Unbelievable and unacceptable.** Floridians know that to protect our small businesses, keep our kids safe in school, and save lives, access to testing is critical. It’s one of the ways we can safely be with our loved ones and move past this pandemic. **Republicans–even Donald Trump–and Democrats alike support access to COVID testing. For some reason, DeSantis and his administration thought we could just pretend like the virus doesn’t exist. Will you take just a moment and share our video to remind everyone what happened on January 6 and recommit to never letting this happen again? [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/40421575/324273073/-2023352048?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzkwMTkwIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImI5NWJiZTI2LTE0NmYtZWMxMS05NGY2LWM4OTY2NTBkOTIzYyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=6z5x_pDJ8uTLV9YlHij2x6R7stGkm14bP8nylrnGpKw=&emci=61498922-086f-ec11-94f6-c896650d923c&emdi=b95bbe26-146f-ec11-94f6-c896650d923c&ceid=16335001) One year ago today, domestic terrorists breached the seat of our government and attempted to destroy our democracy.

**They weren't successful, team.**

But this coup was one of the most terrifying moments in American history. The red flags and warning signs were there. Donald Trump and his allies built up a violent mob that was ready to demolish everything our Founding Fathers worked for.

In the disastrous attack, these people left our Capitol smeared with treason and the stench of death in the air. **Our democracy was under siege for the first time in modern history, all because of Trumpism and American fascism.** While this event will forever be burned in our minds, here's a reminder of what happened one year ago: [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/40421576/324273076/-605454982?v=4176525165780283&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzkwMTkwIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImI5NWJiZTI2LTE0NmYtZWMxMS05NGY2LWM4OTY2NTBkOTIzYyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=6z5x_pDJ8uTLV9YlHij2x6R7stGkm14bP8nylrnGpKw=&emci=61498922-086f-ec11-94f6-c896650d923c&emdi=b95bbe26-146f-ec11-94f6-c896650d923c&ceid=16335001) | [SHARE ON FACEBOOK](https://click.everyaction.com/k/40421577/324273077/989981403?u=https%3A//fb.watch/amLhXrBU8g/&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzkwMTkwIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImI5NWJiZTI2LTE0NmYtZWMxMS05NGY2LWM4OTY2NTBkOTIzYyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=6z5x_pDJ8uTLV9YlHij2x6R7stGkm14bP8nylrnGpKw=&emci=61498922-086f-ec11-94f6-c896650d923c&emdi=b95bbe26-146f-ec11-94f6-c896650d923c&ceid=16335001)

name: Patrick Clark, Keystone Progress
subject: Can you believe it? One year ago today, insurrectionists stormed our Capitol.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Keystone Progress Education Fund, www
body:
Dear Friend,

Can you believe it?

One year ago today, violent Trump supporters stormed the U.S. Capitol in an attempt to overthrow our government and destroy our democracy.

They threatened the lives of our lawmakers and murdered members of the Capitol police. Behind the insurrection and aiding the masses were at least two Pennsylvania lawmakers.

Sen. Doug Mastriano was caught on video leading a crowd of insurrectionists across police lines near the Capitol after the insurrectionists threw aside a police barricade. This man, who was so intent on taking down our government, ironically is now gearing up to run for Governor of our state! We cannot let that happen.

Rep. Scott Perry advised Trump on ways to overturn the results of the free and fair election in 2020 through FALSE claims of voter fraud. Last month, Perry continued to flip off our nation by refusing to cooperate with the Jan. 6 investigation committee, even though he played a key role in encouraging the insurrection.

He and so many other lawmakers in our state — who were elected through the same voting systems — refused to support Pennsylvania voters by disputing the presidential election results. They even went so far as to rack up costly, pointless recounts with the goal of sowing doubt in our elections.

Pennsylvania legislators are still working against voters by gaining control of vote counting and by implementing an anti-majoritarian redistricting map to undermine the State Supreme Court.

Please help us FIGHT BACK with a donation today. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/keystone-progress-now?refcode=010622]

We cannot have these classic villains take down our rights and destroy our nation. We have to protect voters and work to build up a progressive nation.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/keystone-progress-now?refcode=010622 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/keystone-progress-now?refcode=010622]In solidarity,

Patrick Clark
Keystone Progress

name: Josh Riley
subject: My thoughts on the anniversary of January 6
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Josh Riley for Congress
body:
During the course of my career, I’ve been privileged to spend years working in the U.S. Capitol complex.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40421846/324274483/-1126184350?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NGE4NzU4ZC0xNTZmLWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDkyM2MiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=XRg5Re038cG_zJ_JDVtADqfipa0vhtTMQgAm_2_HZcE=&emci=5314b0fb-036f-ec11-94f6-c896650d923c&emdi=44a8758d-156f-ec11-94f6-c896650d923c&ceid=4588596)
---
| During the course of my career, I’ve been privileged to spend years working in the U.S. Capitol complex.

I remember the first day I went to work there as a low-level staffer for Congressman Maurice Hinchey during one of my summers in college. I was in awe of the place, not just because of the grandeur of the buildings — though there is that — but more importantly because of the purpose it served and the public servants it housed.

The purpose it served: a shared space in which men and women from across America could meet to debate big ideas, address big challenges, and achieve big things for folks back home.

The public servants it housed: officials chosen by their communities to give them a voice and deliver results, staff committed to the hard work of studying the issues and guiding the lawmaking process, and so many workers doing the difficult and under-appreciated jobs of keeping the lights on, the hallways clean, and the space and the folks within it protected.

In my experience, the Capitol Police performed their jobs with professionalism, courage, and integrity, not to mention a warm greeting every morning and a smile every night. They were a steady and constant presence, always above the fray of the partisan bickering that took place around them.

When I finished my job on the Senate Judiciary Committee in the fall of 2014, I remember taking note of something as I left: I had the same sense of awe of the place then as I had the first day I went to work there.

Sure, there was every reason not to — every reason to be cynical, frustrated, and discouraged. Partisan gridlock had led to a government shutdown the year before. It had forced changes to the filibuster just to get basic business done. It had resulted in Congress’ failure to act in response to terrible Supreme Court decisions like Citizens United and Shelby County.

Then as now, our country faced many big challenges that Congress had failed to address. In so many respects, our Democracy has not lived up to its ideals.

But, the Capitol still stood as something of a reminder that those ideals remain on the horizon and that we must continue to strive to meet them even when — especially when — we fall short. That’s really important.

And it’s also what made January 6, 2020, so profoundly horrific. The events of that day were an assault not just on a building and the brave men and women in uniform who protect it. It was an assault on American Democracy and the system of government that brings us together as Americans.
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40421847/324274484/-1126184350?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NGE4NzU4ZC0xNTZmLWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDkyM2MiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=XRg5Re038cG_zJ_JDVtADqfipa0vhtTMQgAm_2_HZcE=&emci=5314b0fb-036f-ec11-94f6-c896650d923c&emdi=44a8758d-156f-ec11-94f6-c896650d923c&ceid=4588596)

The insurrection was a shameful and sad moment in which many of us rightly questioned whether our Democracy’s ideals could or would survive at all.

I am hopeful that they will and optimistic that we will be stronger together in the end.

While some continue to perpetuate big lies about the 2020 election and sow seeds of doubt and disinformation about future elections, so many others have come together to condemn the actions that took place on January 6. Our justice system continues the work of accountability. And later this year, voters will have an opportunity to cast ballots in the first federal election since that day — an opportunity to send a message that our Democracy is strong and that we reject those who seek to weaken it.

So, on the anniversary of that awful day, let’s resolve never to take our Democracy for granted and always to work together not only to protect it, but to strengthen it.

With gratitude & high hopes,

Josh Riley

* * *
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40421848/324274485/-1126184350?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NGE4NzU4ZC0xNTZmLWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDkyM2MiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=XRg5Re038cG_zJ_JDVtADqfipa0vhtTMQgAm_2_HZcE=&emci=5314b0fb-036f-ec11-94f6-c896650d923c&emdi=44a8758d-156f-ec11-94f6-c896650d923c&ceid=4588596)

name: Josh Gottheimer
subject: the insurrection
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOSH GOTTHEIMER FOR CONGRESS
body:
# [#]Friend,

On this day last year I lived through the insurrection of our nation's capital, one of the most horrific attacks on our democracy.

As I ran through the tunnels of the capital seeking safety along with other members of congress, I thought of my children, hoping that that morning would not be the last time I told them I loved them. I worried for our nation, how would we ever recover?

The horrific violence that took place in the heart of our democracy should have never been possible. Since then, my colleagues and I have fought on a bipartisan front to hold those involved accountable. Those wanting to tear us down, only made us stronger.

One year later, not enough action has been taken.

We, as a nation, deserve justice for the atrocities that took place on this day last year. I will continue to fight for accountability and the protection of our democracy to ensure that ALL Americans have the opportunities and representation they deserve.

Sincerely,

Josh Gottheimer
Josh Gottheimer represents New Jersey’s Fifth Congressional District. Josh is in Congress to fight for lower taxes and Jersey Values. He’s working across the aisle to find real solutions for New Jersey families and is actively fighting against the ideological gridlock in Washington. Sincerely, Josh Gottheimer Josh Gottheimer represents New Jersey’s Fifth Congressional District. Josh is in Congress to fight for lower taxes and Jersey Values. He’s working across the aisle to find real solutions for New Jersey families and is actively fighting against the ideological gridlock in Washington. name: =?utf-8?Q?Captain=20Michael=20Brown?=
subject: She Did It Again! 😠
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Michael Brown for Governor • P
body:
Any state employee fired or displaced by Whitmer, Nessel, or Benson over the vaccine mandate will be rehired day one after I’m elected. | | | |
---
| | |
---
|
---
| Dear Peter:

She did it again.

Gretchen Whitmer put the jobs and livelihoods of thousands of Michigan workers — and Michigan state employees — on the line when she doubled down on her support of President Biden’s ridiculous vaccine mandate policy.

A couple of weeks ago she seemed to come to her senses telling a group of business owners, "I know if that mandate happens, _we’re going to lose state employees_...it’s going to be a _problem for all of us_.”

But then just before Christmas _**she flip-flopped and came out strongly in support of mandatory vaccinations**_.

I want to be perfectly clear here.

**Any state employee fired or displaced by Whitmer, Nessel, or Benson over the vaccine mandate will be rehired day one after I’m elected.**

The corrections officers, troopers, first responders and other employees have already proven they can serve the citizens without your vaccine mandate.
---
| [ ](https://captainmikeformichigan.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d4c9e98e87232d3682c652313&id=d6f383ffba&e=0636b97700)
---
(click video to play)
| While _Whitmer was busy putting people out of work_ with her lockdown policies, those of us on the frontlines continued to successfully work throughout the pandemic before vaccines became available.

**Peter, we have an historic opportunity to get rid of Gretchen Whitmer and Fix Michigan. Will you join me?**

[Volunteer](https://captainmikeformichigan.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d4c9e98e87232d3682c652313&id=88379c877d&e=0636b97700), [endorse](https://captainmikeformichigan.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d4c9e98e87232d3682c652313&id=2924326f38&e=0636b97700), tell others, and [make a donation](https://captainmikeformichigan.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d4c9e98e87232d3682c652313&id=6bcb82d420&e=0636b97700).

Your donation of [$25](https://captainmikeformichigan.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d4c9e98e87232d3682c652313&id=574770c62e&e=0636b97700), [$50](https://captainmikeformichigan.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d4c9e98e87232d3682c652313&id=01c6266b85&e=0636b97700), or [$500](https://captainmikeformichigan.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d4c9e98e87232d3682c652313&id=d7261a8284&e=0636b97700) will help.

Together we can win this election. Together we can dump Whitmer. _Together we can fix Michigan_.

Will you join me? Michael Brown
Republican for Governor PS - I’m fully vaccinated myself, but I completely support anyone who opts to not get the vaccine because of health, religious, or other reasons.

name: Rebekah Jones for Congress
subject: On this anniversary.... Did we learn our lesson?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rebekah Jones Campaign
body:
On January 6, 2020, we (the Florida Department of Health (DOH) Emergency Preparedness Team I was on) received the first email from the CDC i
| | **Can't see this message?_[View in a browser](https://www.rebekahjonescampaign.com/so/74NuS_k1d/c?w=t9VBPHhYrOZwH5Wj2ppUPNOyMeTOx8MMEg73ql6el84.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cucmViZWthaGpvbmVzY2FtcGFpZ24uY29tL3NvLzc0TnVTX2sxZD9sYW5ndWFnZVRhZz1lbiIsIm0iOiJtYWlsIiwiYyI6IjdlN2E4N2UxLTcwNDMtNDE3OS1iODM4LTVjYTg5MjBmYWFkNyJ9)_**
---
| | |
---
| | Did we learn our lesson?
---
| | On January 6, 2020, we (the Florida Department of Health (DOH) Emergency Preparedness Team I was on) received the first email from the CDC issuing official guidance about the pneumonia outbreak the WHO was monitoring in Wuhan, China. My husband – a biostatistician and perpetual hypochondriac – sent me an article a week earlier about the same topic, so this wasn’t the first I had heard of this outbreak, but the first time we at DOH received any official word about what to expect. Throughout January, we were told to begin planning for a pandemic, but we did not. I spent the next year tracking and reporting on COVID-19 in my home state of Florida. That journey became a public affair in May when I was outed to the world by Ron Desantis (in front of the vice president) as a whistleblower before I even spoke publicly about what was happening or even officially filed my complaint with the state. I asked my boss for information on how to go about filing the complaint the Friday afternoon before I was told either to resign or be terminated the following Monday. I had until that Thursday to make a decision. Desantis went on his violent rant that Wednesday. Since that time, my life has become somewhat of a public spectacle. I did my best to shield and protect my family from the threats and abuse hurled at me online. I never shared photos of my husband to protect his identity, as he was (and still is) working for the state. 99% of the feedback was positive and full of love and support. I became an awkward and reluctant celebrity-of-sorts in the process, representing oppressed scientists across the nation. The accolades, awards, invitations to speak, news appearances, and the like came rushing in like a flood, and I worked as hard as I could to stay on message and make sure people had the information they needed to make informed decisions. Then came the raid on my home. DeSantis’ goal of silencing me by threatening my children at gunpoint backfired splendidly. I went from a mostly Florida-centric sciencelebrity, to a national symbol with a major platform with which to challenge him for being the monster he is. He amplified my voice in a way I never imagined possible. But I no longer felt safe in our home in Florida. My efforts to protect my own family failed. I watched the police go through my entire house, demanding several times to see the warrant and call my attorney, as I was told I had a right to do, but they didn’t have the warrant when they pointed a gun at my head and told me to open the door. It came several hours later. They handed the warrant to me as they were leaving. I had no rights. I stood my ground. I defied a governor whose brittle ego made him obsessed with me and a handful of my outspoken and defiant friends (Dan Uhlfelder, Tom Kennedy, and Grant Stern to name a few). I paid a price for that. Desantis engineered a smear campaign earlier this year designed solely to discredit me so that he wouldn't look like the evil man that he is for raiding my home. Though none of it was ever true, and some of it is flat-out ridiculous, a lie can travel halfway across the world before the truth even laces up its boots. Desantis has lied about the pandemic since the start. Attacking me felt like an extension of that. Though even I didn't realize how obsessed he had become with my success in standing up to his lies. Last summer, my lawyers and I submitted a public records request to the Governor’s office for any emails about me between Desantis, his inner circle, and a handful of outside entities he used to fight his war or science (bloggers at the Nationalist Review, the FDLE agents involved in the raid, Twitter executives, and a few others). They have yet to provide those documents, but did give us a number on how many there were between May 2020 – June 2021. I asked my friend Grant to take a wild guess – he’s gone through countless rounds of records request and would have a good grasp on what was normal or not. He guessed a few hundred. The actual number? 4,300. That’s just the emails. It does not include texts, phone calls, in-person conversations, or anything else. Just the emails about me. That averaged out to about 11 emails a day. Every single day. For more than a year. If I had known how sick Desantis’ obsession about me had become, I would have sent my family away to protect them. If I had known he was so singularly fixated on silencing me, I would have taken steps to keep us safe. We fled Florida and moved to Washington, D.C. as de-facto political asylum seekers just weeks after the raid. We moved to the DC area on January 4, 2021. Two days later, crazy followed us and a crowd of insurrectionists not only attacked the capital, but also put the entire city on lockdown. We thought DC was a war zone with the constant sirens, helicopters, and warnings to seek shelter inside due to possible terrorist attacks. Only after several months of living in refuge there did we realize how absolutely insane that day was. Nearly six months later, much to the surprise of the entire nation, the Florida DOH Inspector General’s office found my whistleblower complaint credible and issued me full whistleblower protection. That would have been nice to have before armed police raided my home and pointed guns at my two-year-old daughter and 11-year-old son. I realized the day I received that letter that this mysterious timeline for when we could come home to Florida (maybe after Desantis’ lost his re-election, maybe sometime after that) was now clear. We could move back home now. I still don’t know how safe it is, though. While there would be serious repercussions for any continued retaliation against me because of my protected whistleblower status, we’re talking about a man devoid of morality and fear of consequence. Desantis continues to break the law, endanger and kill our people with his intentionally dangerous policies, and does so publicly, brazenly and without remorse. Oddly enough, even my opponent, a man under investigation for a host of illegal activity, including sex trafficking minors, recognizes Desantis for the monster he is. Gaetz once acknowledged to a good friend of mine how Desantis is a “sociopath.” In Gaetz’ own words, “you can’t teach empathy.” Coming from a man who sexually exploits, drugs and traffics high school students, that’s a pretty strong statement of damnation. Of course Gaetz once called himself the Robin to Desantis’ batman, and asked Desantis to be his best man at his wedding (Desantis didn’t go to the wedding at all in the end), so I wouldn’t place bets on Gaetz standing by his own word on that. Today, two years after the first formal notice of COVID-19, and one year from the insurrection and riots at our nation’s capital, and collectively we seem too exhausted, too disappointed, too un-eager to band together, stand our ground, and fight back. I’m here to tell you that 2022 will be the year real Americans fight back. This will be the year we oust monsters like Desantis and Gaetz, and demand better from our leaders. This will be a year of action. It has to be. Because we stand to lose too much if we don’t. Join our fight! Help us send a message that the radicalization of American politics will not be tolerated here in Florida, or anywhere in our great nation. We are too great in number and too strong of character to let these atrocities go unanswered. The way we fight back is with our wallets and at the voting booth. So let’s get it done. [Stand together, stand strong, and stand your ground in 2022](https://www.rebekahjonescampaign.com/so/74NuS_k1d/c?w=bhWSgp94TZIgMRU5pUypL4Flr3GVsahrAyFIxmk6Euw.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYWN0Ymx1ZS5jb20vZG9uYXRlL2ZpZ2h0MjAyMiIsInIiOiJiMTU4YzJkNC03MWM0LTRiMGQtYjYzOS04NzQyYTJmODM3N2EiLCJtIjoibWFpbCIsImMiOiI3ZTdhODdlMS03MDQzLTQxNzktYjgzOC01Y2E4OTIwZmFhZDcifQ). The future of our state and our nation depends on it.

clean_body: On January 6, 2020, we (the Florida Department of Health (DOH) Emergency Preparedness Team I was on) received the first email from the CDC i | | Can't see this message? | | | | | Did we learn our lesson? | | On January 6, 2020, we (the Florida Department of Health (DOH) Emergency Preparedness Team I was on) received the first email from the CDC issuing official guidance about the pneumonia outbreak the WHO was monitoring in Wuhan, China. My husband – a biostatistician and perpetual hypochondriac – sent me an article a week earlier about the same topic, so this wasn’t the first I had heard of this outbreak, but the first time we at DOH received any official word about what to expect. Throughout January, we were told to begin planning for a pandemic, but we did not. I spent the next year tracking and reporting on COVID-19 in my home state of Florida. That journey became a public affair in May when I was outed to the world by Ron Desantis (in front of the vice president) as a whistleblower before I even spoke publicly about what was happening or even officially filed my complaint with the state. I asked my boss for information on how to go about filing the complaint the Friday afternoon before I was told either to resign or be terminated the following Monday. I had until that Thursday to make a decision. Desantis went on his violent rant that Wednesday. Since that time, my life has become somewhat of a public spectacle. I did my best to shield and protect my family from the threats and abuse hurled at me online. I never shared photos of my husband to protect his identity, as he was (and still is) working for the state. 99% of the feedback was positive and full of love and support. I became an awkward and reluctant celebrity-of-sorts in the process, representing oppressed scientists across the nation. The accolades, awards, invitations to speak, news appearances, and the like came rushing in like a flood, and I worked as hard as I could to stay on message and make sure people had the information they needed to make informed decisions. Then came the raid on my home. DeSantis’ goal of silencing me by threatening my children at gunpoint backfired splendidly. I went from a mostly Florida-centric sciencelebrity, to a national symbol with a major platform with which to challenge him for being the monster he is. He amplified my voice in a way I never imagined possible. But I no longer felt safe in our home in Florida. My efforts to protect my own family failed. I watched the police go through my entire house, demanding several times to see the warrant and call my attorney, as I was told I had a right to do, but they didn’t have the warrant when they pointed a gun at my head and told me to open the door. It came several hours later. They handed the warrant to me as they were leaving. I had no rights. I stood my ground. I defied a governor whose brittle ego made him obsessed with me and a handful of my outspoken and defiant friends (Dan Uhlfelder, Tom Kennedy, and Grant Stern to name a few). I paid a price for that. Desantis engineered a smear campaign earlier this year designed solely to discredit me so that he wouldn't look like the evil man that he is for raiding my home. Though none of it was ever true, and some of it is flat-out ridiculous, a lie can travel halfway across the world before the truth even laces up its boots. Desantis has lied about the pandemic since the start. Attacking me felt like an extension of that. Though even I didn't realize how obsessed he had become with my success in standing up to his lies. Last summer, my lawyers and I submitted a public records request to the Governor’s office for any emails about me between Desantis, his inner circle, and a handful of outside entities he used to fight his war or science (bloggers at the Nationalist Review, the FDLE agents involved in the raid, Twitter executives, and a few others). They have yet to provide those documents, but did give us a number on how many there were between May 2020 – June 2021. I asked my friend Grant to take a wild guess – he’s gone through countless rounds of records request and would have a good grasp on what was normal or not. He guessed a few hundred. The actual number? 4,300. That’s just the emails. It does not include texts, phone calls, in-person conversations, or anything else. Just the emails about me. That averaged out to about 11 emails a day. Every single day. For more than a year. If I had known how sick Desantis’ obsession about me had become, I would have sent my family away to protect them. If I had known he was so singularly fixated on silencing me, I would have taken steps to keep us safe. That would have been nice to have before armed police raided my home and pointed guns at my two-year-old daughter and 11-year-old son. Of course Gaetz once called himself the Robin to Desantis’ batman, and asked Desantis to be his best man at his wedding (Desantis didn’t go to the wedding at all in the end), so I wouldn’t place bets on Gaetz standing by his own word on that. Today, we remember a dark day in American history – **the January 6th insurrection at our nation’s Capitol.**
What happened on January 6th, 2021 was a stark reminder that lies perpetuated in this country can lead to real violence.
**And the biggest lie is that America is at war with itself.**

But before we get back to trusting one another, we need to make sure that those responsible for the events that took place on January 6th are held accountable. We also need to ensure that the Republicans perpetuating Trump’s Big Lie, like Charles’ opponents, never set foot in Congress.

name: Mark Holland
subject: A call to truth and courage
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Holland for Kansas
body:
Mark Holland is running for US Senate because Kansas and America deserve a strong, courageous leader who leads with faith, speaks the truth, and is willing to stand up to extremists across the political spectrum. If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://email.ngpvan.com/unsubscribeUnique/da5dd659-196f-ec11-94f6-c896650d923c/bcda659f-1a6f-ec11-94f6-c896650d923c?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzM1NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiY2RhNjU5Zi0xYTZmLWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDkyM2MiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1sJgulfiCvqKhMLmvib4ci23wMErz2pFAP_w02RMIBc=&id=103697760)
---
| Home of the brave.

We all watched on live television as rioters assaulted police officers, smashed windows, and violently broke into our nation’s Capital. This riot is what American politics has become, and will continue to be, if we do not take action to defend our democracy.

January 6 was the culmination of lies. Donald Trump began his term as President lying about the size of his inauguration crowd. A small, obviously wrong, meaningless lie that foretold the greater lies to come. He encapsulated all of his lies by declaring, “What you’re seeing and what you’re reading is not what is happening.” He staked his legitimacy on “alternative facts” and “fake news.” His greatest lie was that the 2020 election was stolen from him (it was not), and this lie led directly to the January 6 riot.

January 6 was the culmination of cowardice. Love him or hate him, Donald Trump leads with intimidation and fear. His personal attacks and scorched-earth tactics have defined his entire business and political life. Trump’s lies would be meaningless if those around him had the courage to stand up to him. Instead, the vast majority of Republican politicians, including Kansas Senator Jerry Moran, are dependent on Trump’s popularity for their own fundraising and re-elections. January 6 would never have happened if Republicans in the House of Representatives and the Senate had the courage to hold Trump accountable from day one.

January 6 is a call to truth and courage. The very freedom we enjoy in this country is dependent on truth. And truth is dependent on courage. I am running for US Senate in Kansas because our state and our nation need a leader who will speak the truth, even when it is unpopular. We need community servants who are more concerned about the greater good than their own reelection. We need politicians who will stand up to their own party when it is wrong.

[We need to protect our democracy and stand up for truth and courage, that's why I'm running for Senate. If you can, please support my campaign today.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40422844/324279547/-1390744932?amount=10&refcode=MH_EM_FR_1MO_220106_1_1&refcode2=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzM1NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiY2RhNjU5Zi0xYTZmLWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDkyM2MiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1sJgulfiCvqKhMLmvib4ci23wMErz2pFAP_w02RMIBc=)

Rev. Mark Holland
Former Mayor of Kansas City, Kansas
Kansas Democrat Candidate for US Senate

**Meet Mark:** Reverend Mark Holland is a fourth generation Kansan and a third generation United Methodist Pastor. He has dedicated his life to the service of others, as a father, pastor, community leader, elected official, and non-profit leader. Mark is the co-founder and executive director of Mainstream UMC, a global advocacy organization dedicated to greater inclusion in the church. He has served the Church in Kansas for over 25 years, most recently as the Senior Pastor of Trinity Community Church in Kansas City, Kansas for 19 years.

name: Michael Bennet
subject: January 6
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bennet for Colorado
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40422806/324279422/721416609?refcode=header&amount=25&amounts=10,25,50,100,250,500,1000&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZjBlYTg3YS0xYTZmLWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDkyM2MiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=XwVRR-c6MWvLfDejQXKdTyV1CUHEVzwF-PBay6F8u4A=)
One year ago today, violent rioters overtook the United States Capitol on what became one of the darkest days in our nation’s history. Driven by Donald Trump's lies, egged on by National Republicans, and propelled by conspiracy theories on the internet, insurrectionists assaulted our democracy. Congress returned that night to fulfill our constitutional duty and affirmed President Biden’s victory, but the day serves as a stark reminder of our ongoing responsibility to defend and uphold our democracy. And our fight is not over. Our democracy is still at risk one year later as so many, including some of my opponents, refuse to acknowledge that Joe Biden won fair and square. I will never stop fighting to protect your right to vote. When my Republican colleagues deny the results of the 2020 election, they’re denying the voices of the 159 million Americans who voted — including those who voted for them. As Americans, we cannot disregard our Constitution just because we don’t like an election’s outcome. When they sow doubt in the integrity of our electoral process, they open the floodgates of suppressive voting laws and their intentions become clear as day. We owe our children and grandchildren a stronger democracy that works better for all Americans, and an economy that works for everyone, not just those at the very top. Our democratic example has always shined brightest when we stood for both freedom and opportunity; one idea cannot meaningfully exist without the other. I will never stop fighting to leave our democracy stronger for generations to come; it’s the work of our lifetimes. Yours in the fight, Michael |
---
Bennet for Colorado
PO Box 3078
Denver, CO 80201
United States

Peter,

One year ago today, a violent pro-Trump mob stormed our nation's Capitol building as Kim and her colleagues met to certify the results of the 2020 presidential election.

January 6, 2021 will be remembered as one of the darkest days for our nation's democracy, but it didn't occur in a vacuum. For months leading up to the attack, Donald Trump and his allies peddled baseless claims that the election was rigged.

**And even after we saw the dangerous consequences of these falsehoods, Kim's Republican opponents are _still_ amplifying Trump's “Big Lie!”**

One of Kim's opponents wouldn’t say whether President Joe Biden’s win over Donald Trump was legitimate. Another baselessly claimed "absolutely there was fraud in this election and that should be investigated."

It has been shown time and again that the 2020 presidential election was free and fair. Kim's opponents' refusal to reject the "Big Lie" is dangerous and undermines our democracy. If we want to ensure an event like the January 6 Capitol insurrection never happens again, we need every Republican to take a stand against Trump's fictitious and dangerous election fraud claims.

name: Rand Paul
subject: big news!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rand Paul for Senate |
body:
Friend, in just the last few years, we’ve seen liberal government wreak havoc on our way of life. [](https://click.americanupdate.com/Pages/BC.aspx?ghff34ghj=01%7c210334%7c8394%7c8%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz%7c805&wdmdk=aDKGdjtg4PcqWEjcGtSKEA&sysid=1)
[](https://click.americanupdate.com/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=01%7c11049%7c210334%7c805%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=t9IH4MEbJFY%2bP%2bezr8uOrQ&sysid=1) | |
---
Friend, in just the last few years, we’ve seen liberal government wreak havoc on our way of life.

From nationwide rioting and violent protests to unconstitutional lockdowns, to mask mandates and record-setting inflation, Americans are facing difficult times.

And an out-of-control, Democrat-led Congress and liberal president in the White House isn’t helping. Instead of providing solutions, they continue to pour billions of taxpayer dollars into pet projects and bad investments.

That’s why today, I’m officially announcing my campaign for reelection to serve the people of Kentucky - and the whole liberty movement - in the Senate for another term.

**[STAND WITH RAND: CHIP IN TO SUPPORT RE-ELECTION >>](https://click.americanupdate.com/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=01%7c11040%7c210334%7c805%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=KREl6PVGMB04FpyJt2jAjQ&sysid=1)**

I know this race won’t be easy -- we have tough challenges ahead.
** **
The bureaucrats in our government, the pro-censorship leaders in big-tech, and the anti-freedom liberals in the media will do anything they can to make sure we don’t succeed.
** **
That’s why I want to start off with a big goal \- to make sure we’re prepared for whatever comes next. I’m pledging today to raise $100,000 for the launch of this campaign and I hope you’ll join me by chipping in now.
__
[**LAUNCH DONATION: $15**](https://click.americanupdate.com/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=01%7c11041%7c210334%7c805%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=dWjaBH0hAeE%2fYgw3tx7QMg&sysid=1)
[**LAUNCH DONATION: $25**](https://click.americanupdate.com/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=01%7c11042%7c210334%7c805%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=ibe6M4m30AEoFKz25U%2fDXA&sysid=1)
[**LAUNCH DONATION: $35**](https://click.americanupdate.com/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=01%7c11043%7c210334%7c805%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=4TBSRrhZJe1IyWHqSZLAYg&sysid=1)
[**LAUNCH DONATION: $45**](https://click.americanupdate.com/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=01%7c11044%7c210334%7c805%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=pR4J6n4s3BSgrnbqdZDonQ&sysid=1)
The stakes are just too high to let the liberals maintain control in Washington.

You’re already feeling the damage they’ve done!

* Gas prices are up 40%
* Groceries cost up to 6% more
* Rent, cars, and energy prices are skyrocketing
* _Americans are struggling. _

It’s hitting us hard all over the nation.

And here in Kentucky, where we’re recovering not only from a pandemic but a statewide natural disaster, we simply cannot afford to continue down this path.

We must correct the course now. And we can do that by taking back the Senate and restoring a common-sense, pro-freedom approach to government.

**_Let’s not forget: we only need one seat to take control. _**

And I’m 100% confident that if we stand together now we can take a stand for liberty and win that one seat to take back the Senate in 2022.

Please, chip in what you can to make our launch a success and start this year off the right way: **[https://secure.winred.com/randpaul/action-3476970/](https://click.americanupdate.com/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=01%7c11155%7c210334%7c805%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=5ixk47k878Rnl4I0%2bAViRQ&sysid=1)**

Thank you in advance.

In liberty,
Rand Paul
__

subject: Chris' statement on January 6th
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY Van Hollen for Senate
body:
| |
---
Today is the one-year anniversary of the deadly attack on our Capitol, and our democracy remains in danger. [Add your name here if you agree that we must redouble our work in 2022 to defend against Donald Trump and a Republican Party that continues to condone and amplify his attacks on our democracy!](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40423654/324283086/-124357146?refcode=CVH_EM_OT_3MO_220106_1_1&refcode2=00eb054e-1c6f-ec11-94f6-c896650d923c&contactdata=kxX5tKoLxF1mP%2Fe2EU7BuTskUnteyyqkdetWW4RZctG%2BJyzDphSgxTGIx3NK6uci&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODIzMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMGViMDU0ZS0xYzZmLWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDkyM2MiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-NsZoWKMWMiT28DbwuHtsw0PY-RP8gQKpB5IOfptK3c=&emci=756aa01f-156f-ec11-94f6-c896650d923c&emdi=00eb054e-1c6f-ec11-94f6-c896650d923c&ceid=2216363) Read Chris’ statement on the one-year anniversary below: _**\----Begin Chris' Message----**_ From: Chris Van Hollen
Date: Wed, Jan 5, 2022
Subject: one year later, our democracy remains in peril The January 6th attack on our Capitol was an unprecedented attack on the peaceful certification of our democratic election results incited by a sitting president in an effort to retain power illegally. It was, to be blunt, an attempted coup. And it will go down in history as a critical juncture for our democracy. As we learn more about the events leading up to and including that day, it’s clear the outcome was far from certain. Had it not been for the Capitol Hill police and heroes like Officer Goodman, as well as the D.C. police and the National Guard, the certification vote might have been stopped. It’s an alarming reality that one year later the threat to our democracy has only grown. While the Capitol itself is now more secure, our democracy isn’t. The Republican Party has refused to denounce the Big Lie that fed the attack. Instead, when the coup at the Capitol failed, Republican-controlled state legislatures began passing voter suppression laws across the country that are especially designed to make it more difficult for people of color and younger voters to access the ballot box — and even include provisions that empower partisan officials to overturn the people's vote. These laws represent nothing less than a coordinated attempt to usurp our electoral process and gain undue influence over the outcome of the upcoming 2022 and 2024 elections. The failure of Republican senators to convict Donald Trump at his impeachment trial for his role in the January 6 attack — and for his failure to use his powers to stop the violence — was unconscionable. But the Republican party's actions since that time may be even more destructive for our future. Over the last year the Republican party, with a handful of exceptions, has overwhelmingly made it clear that going forward they will stand with Donald Trump over our Constitution and the rule of law. Like Trump's compatriot, Prime Minister Viktor Orban in Hungary, whose cruel and authoritarian rule Trump just publicly endorsed again yesterday, they have turned their back on democratic principles. Never since the civil war has our democracy been in such peril. That's why this is a moment that calls for decisive action. January is the make or break month for passing the Freedom to Vote Act and the John Lewis Voting Rights Enhancement Act two measures critical to protecting our democratic electoral process. Republicans oppose both measures. That is why it is essential that we muster fifty Senate Democratic votes to reform the filibuster rule. The current rule runs contrary to the original vision of the Senate as a place where we have vigorous and long debates, but where a majority vote ultimately prevails. We must reform our filibuster rules to achieve that original intent and protect our democracy. This will require complete unanimity in our Senate Democratic Caucus and we will vote on or before January 17th. While we survived January 6th, our democracy is still very much at risk. We can't afford to wait in taking action. History is watching and will remember how we responded at this critical juncture. Onwards, Chris
* * *
| Grassroots supporters like you help our campaign make the investments
it will take to re-elect Chris in 2022 and protect our Democratic Senate majority: | Had it not been for the Capitol Hill police and heroes like Officer Goodman, as well as the D.C. police and the National Guard, the certification vote might have been stopped. The Republican Party has refused to denounce the Big Lie that fed the attack. Instead, when the coup at the Capitol failed, Republican-controlled state legislatures began passing voter suppression laws across the country that are especially designed to make it more difficult for people of color and younger voters to access the ballot box — and even include provisions that empower partisan officials to overturn the people's vote. These laws represent nothing less than a coordinated attempt to usurp our electoral process and gain undue influence over the outcome of the upcoming 2022 and 2024 elections. The failure of Republican senators to convict Donald Trump at his impeachment trial for his role in the January 6 attack — and for his failure to use his powers to stop the violence — was unconscionable. But the Republican party's actions since that time may be even more destructive for our future. Over the last year the Republican party, with a handful of exceptions, has overwhelmingly made it clear that going forward they will stand with Donald Trump over our Constitution and the rule of law. Like Trump's compatriot, Prime Minister Viktor Orban in Hungary, whose cruel and authoritarian rule Trump just publicly endorsed again yesterday, they have turned their back on democratic principles. Never since the civil war has our democracy been in such peril. That's why this is a moment that calls for decisive action. January is the make or break month for passing the Freedom to Vote Act and the John Lewis Voting Rights Enhancement Act two measures critical to protecting our democratic electoral process. Republicans oppose both measures. That is why it is essential that we muster fifty Senate Democratic votes to reform the filibuster rule. The current rule runs contrary to the original vision of the Senate as a place where we have vigorous and long debates, but where a majority vote ultimately prevails. We must reform our filibuster rules to achieve that original intent and protect our democracy. This will require complete unanimity in our Senate Democratic Caucus and we will vote on or before January 17th. While we survived January 6th, our democracy is still very much at risk. We can't afford to wait in taking action. History is watching and will remember how we responded at this critical juncture. Onwards, Chris * | Grassroots supporters like you help our campaign make the investments it will take to re-elect Chris in 2022 and protect our Democratic Senate majority: | = | = ---|--- PAID FOR BY Van Hollen for Senate Van Hollen for Senate | 10605 Concord Street, Suite 202 | Kensington, MD, 20895 If you need to, click to receive only our most important emails or you can unsubscribe here: You can also =.

name: Victoria Virasingh
subject: I can’t believe it’s been a year.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Victoria for Congress
body:
A year ago today, armed insurrectionists attacked the U.S. Capitol Building.

| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40423930/324284339/686715116?refcode=220106_CLTV_january6_LOGO&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTYyNiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjN2Q5MDA0NS0xZTZmLWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDkyM2MiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bWxPm5nrF1Wjfy_P-7YvVNHMHUi4qtI-qqRelpujoEU=)
---
Peter,

**January 6th.** Another date that just by its name, Americans feel the gravity of the traumatic events that occurred on that day.

A year ago today, armed insurrectionists attacked the U.S. Capitol Building. The violence inflicted wasn’t from outside threats but domestic terrorists — extremists fueled by hatred determined to undermine our sacred democratic system.

The lies and malicious rhetoric of Donald Trump have forever tainted the structure of our government, and the terrifying events of January 6, 2020 demonstrate how dangerous it is when this type of bigotry and radical conspiracy culminates.

Between 2006 and 2011, I interned on the Hill. I remember my first time entering the Capitol Building and feeling an overwhelming sense of pride. To watch our nation’s capital be forcibly stormed by violent radicals last year was completely heartbreaking.

The treasured landmarks in D.C. do not just house our federal offices, they represent our entire country’s pillars of democracy and the freedoms we have as Americans. They represent our strength, our dignity, and our resilience.

So much of that was shattered during last year’s Jan. 6 insurrection, but it only means that we must unite together and fight harder than ever to restore what has been broken and rebuild faith in our government. In solidarity, **_Victoria_**
* * *

Far-right extremists left a scar on our nation so deep that we’re still scrambling to recover. While we, the Democrats, work together to prevent another attack on our democracy, Republicans have only doubled down to undermine it. I am talking about their egregious voter suppression efforts to disenfranchise Black and brown communities. I am talking about the GOP rooting for Donald Trump’s return in 2024, and the numerous Republicans who STILL embrace the Big Lie AND play down the January 6th insurrection. Folks, if you feel like our country is going to hell in a handbasket, you’re not alone. clean_body: This is about the future of our democracy. | Friend, I know you’re well aware that today is the one-year anniversary of Donald Trump’s failed coup attempt in Washington. Far-right extremists left a scar on our nation so deep that we’re still scrambling to recover. While we, the Democrats, work together to prevent another attack on our democracy, Republicans have only doubled down to undermine it. I am talking about their egregious voter suppression efforts to disenfranchise Black and brown communities. I am talking about the GOP rooting for Donald Trump’s return in 2024, and the numerous Republicans who STILL embrace the Big Lie AND play down the January 6th insurrection. Folks, if you feel like our country is going to hell in a handbasket, you’re not alone. But there is hope. We just need to work together harder than ever to hold Republicans accountable for their betrayal — at the ballot box. I announced my candidacy for Congress because I couldn’t take it anymore. It doesn’t take a scientist like me to know that January 6th was a small glimpse of what’s ahead for the American people in 2022 and 2024 if we don’t step up immediately. No more games. No more sitting on the sidelines. Let’s show the far-right movement how angry we are by joining me to hold the GOP accountable for January 6th — and save the future of our democracy >>> Thanks for your support, Matt = = PAID FOR BY Matt West for Congress Matt West for Congress PO Box 1926 Beaverton, OR 97075 United States Click here to

body:
| [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40424070/324285120/145944154?refcode=header-img&amount=25&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTMvMS85MDUzMCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZDNkNDFhZS0xZTZmLWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDkyM2MiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EtjIEP92IlJPkS9u0C_agqOmPdBLDHVeYi3YqnQcNsk=)

Peter, this is a hard email for me to write.

**Today marks one year since the violent, treasonous, Trump-incited insurrection on the Capitol** — _and on the very fabric of our democracy._

**January 6th was one of the darkest days in our country’s recent history.** Trump’s followers nearly overthrew the democratic, historic proceeding of certifying the results of President Biden’s win.

I am grateful to the members of Congress, aides, first responders, and all who fought for — _and saved_ — our democracy on that day.

**But as we reflect on this grim anniversary, we face another election.**

The midterm elections, and Special Elections across the country, are a true test of where we as a nation will go from the aftermath of January 6th.

It’s clear to me, and I hope it is clear to you: **We must bolster our slim Democratic majorities in the House and the Senate with more Democrats who will fight for what’s right in the face of Trump’s Republicans.**

We can’t leave this up to chance, Peter. It is my promise to you that I will defend democracy, my constituents, and all Americans against the hatred of the MAGA Republican Party. [**Please consider chipping in $3 to my campaign today.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40424071/324285121/982711125?amount=3&refcode=PAFC_20220106_EMDF_E1_T0_AC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTMvMS85MDUzMCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZDNkNDFhZS0xZTZmLWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDkyM2MiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EtjIEP92IlJPkS9u0C_agqOmPdBLDHVeYi3YqnQcNsk=) | [CONTRIBUTE $3 »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/40424072/324285122/982711125?amount=3&refcode=PAFC_20220106_EMDF_E1_T0_AC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTMvMS85MDUzMCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZDNkNDFhZS0xZTZmLWVjMTEtOTRmNi1jODk2NjUwZDkyM2MiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EtjIEP92IlJPkS9u0C_agqOmPdBLDHVeYi3YqnQcNsk=)

Thank you,

Phil Arballo
| | Paid for by Arballo For Congress P.O. Box 3406, Fresno, CA 93650
www.philarballo.com

Phil Arballo is a Latino, a Central Valley native, a father, and a husband. He's running for Congress in California to replace Devin Nunes -- a far-right, MAGA Republican who just abandoned the Central Valley. Phil is a champion for real working families and will fight to protect health care access, keep guns out of the wrong hands, and stand up to traitorous Republicans who are working to ruin our Democracy.
